articles.title: Tech Stocks Slip as Traders Await Hormuz Update: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: A retreat in technology shares pulled stocks lower in the US and Asia. Oil fell as traders waited for signs of progress toward a peace deal in the Middle East.

articles.title: UK Energy Price Cap Seen Surging 13% in Biggest Gain Since 2023
articles.summary: The UK’s cap on household energy bills is set to jump 13% this summer as the war in Iran drives up wholesale gas and power costs, adding to broader inflationary pressures weighing on the economy.

articles.title: Indonesian Markets Slump on Commodity Export Control Speculation
articles.summary: Indonesian markets fell as speculation mounted that the government will centralize commodity exports to control capital flows and shore up a plunging currency. Palm oil futures rose.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO Adds Second Musk Stock. It’s a Problem for Tesla
articles.summary: For years, there was only one way for mom-and-pop investors to buy into Elon Musk’s vision: shares of Tesla Inc. That’s about to change — and it’s a serious risk for Tesla investors.

articles.title: Indra CEO to Leave in Next Shakeup at Spain Defense Champion
articles.summary: Indra Sistemas SA, the defense company backed by the Spanish government, is set to replace its top executive in the latest management shakeup after its controversial chairman stepped down last month.

articles.title: Global Bond Selloff Threatens Turmoil in Weakest Asian Economies
articles.summary: Three of Asia’s most vulnerable economies are showing rising strains as their central banks come under pressure to tighten policy even as the economic hit from the Iran-war oil shock deepens.
